ACH.notes: Standard ACH: next-business-day. Same-day ACH available for premium; settling weekdays only. Nacha rules apply. Returns possible up to 60 days for unauthorized debits (R10).

Zelle.notes: Limits vary by bank: Chase $2,500/transaction, $5,000/day; Bank of America $2,500/day. Instant settlement means funds are immediately accessible. No chargeback path for authorized push payments — key AML vulnerability.

wire_domestic.notes: Fedwire. Same-day if sent before 5pm ET. No practical max (Fedwire handles RTGS). Recall possible only with beneficiary bank cooperation — not guaranteed. Commonly used for extraction.

wire_international_SWIFT.notes: 3–5 business day settlement typical for SWIFT; correspondent bank fees $15–$50. SWIFT gpi enables same-day for premium corridors. Jurisdictions with FATF grey/blacklist status add delays. AML screening at each correspondent hop.

Interac_eTransfer.notes: Canadian domestic. Autodeposit makes cancellation impossible after acceptance. No chargeback for authorized push. Limits vary by FI: RBC up to $3,000/transaction; limit increases possible for business accounts.

card_debit.notes: Visa/Mastercard debit interchange; authorization is instant, settlement T+1 to T+2. $2,500 typical daily POS limit; ATM sub-limits apply separately. Chargebacks possible under Reg E for unauthorized transactions.

card_credit.notes: Visa/Mastercard credit; authorization instant, settlement T+1 to T+2. Credit limit determines effective max. Chargebacks under Reg Z up to 120 days. CNP (card-not-present) fraud more common in synthetic ID scenarios.

RTP_FedNow.notes: FedNow and RTP (The Clearing House): instant, 24/7/365. $500,000 per-transaction max. No recall once accepted. Rapidly growing adoption — increasingly used in fraud scenarios due to irreversibility and speed.

crypto_bitcoin.notes: No per-transaction limit. ~10-minute block times; 6 confirmations (~60 min) considered final. High-value transactions may wait for more confirmations. Chain analytics firms (Chainalysis, Elliptic) can trace flows; mixers/tumblers used for obfuscation.

crypto_monero.notes: Privacy coin; ring signatures and stealth addresses make tracing practically impossible. Preferred for extraction in sophisticated operations. Some exchanges refuse XMR deposits due to AML pressure.

crypto_stablecoin_USDC_USDT.notes: USDT/USDC on Ethereum/TRON; near-instant on TRON (low fees). USDT issuer (Tether) can freeze flagged addresses. USDC (Circle) also has freeze capability. Transparent on-chain but stable value makes them preferred for large layering.

cash_ATM.notes: ATM daily limits typically $500–$1,000 for personal accounts, up to $3,000 for premium. Cash leaves no counterparty record. CTR required for cash transactions >$10,000. Structuring (multiple withdrawals to stay below CTR) is a federal crime under 31 U.S.C. § 5324.

prepaid_card.notes: Vanilla Visa, Green Dot, etc. Minimal KYC for low-value cards (<$500 load). Used to break traceability — loaded with mule funds and spent or converted. BSA regulations now require ID for reloadable cards.
